Algeria.status: Tied with Austria on points but behind on GD (-2 vs 0) — needs a win to take clear control of 2nd place

Austria.status: Tied with Algeria on points, ahead on GD — a draw keeps them ahead of Algeria on GD

ALG.NOTE: Algeria's SOT/corner output is highly bimodal — almost nothing vs elite Argentina (1 SOT, 2 corners), excellent vs weaker Jordan (8 SOT, 10 corners). Their genuinely low, consistent trait is FOULS (8, then 6) — a controlled, technical team regardless of opponent quality.

AUT.PRIOR_LESSON_SABITZER: ARG-AUT Q9 was a -31.86 loss: we applied RULE15 (extreme-underdog forward suppression) to Sabitzer when Austria was an 8.7% underdog fighting hard to come back from a deficit — but 'competitive chasing context = more shots,' not fewer. RULE15 only holds when the underdog DISENGAGES (genuine blowout); a team fighting for something generates MORE shot attempts from its creative outlet, not less.
AUT.PRIOR_LESSON_CORNERS_AND_CARDS: ARG-AUT Q4 (-19.91) and Q6 (-19.17): both 'Austria corners' and 'total cards' were modeled assuming a blowout/dominant-win pattern from AUT-JOR, but ARG-AUT was a genuinely competitive, close match — chasing teams accumulate corners from repelled attacks, and competitive matches produce MORE cards than the dominant-win comparison suggested.

context_flags: RULE8_MAXIMUM: HT/FT draw probability is 47.17% — the highest of ANY GD3 fixture analyzed this entire session. This is a textbook cagey, even, high-stakes match between two teams tied on points.

TRUE_COIN_FLIP_MATCHUP: FTR is essentially 26.5/25.8/47.2 — the most evenly-matched fixture of the day. Critically, this means Austria is NOT in the extreme-underdog role that caused the Sabitzer/corners/cards lessons to fail in ARG-AUT — apply those lessons' DIRECTION (don't over-suppress a competitive team) but recognize today's baseline is even more balanced, so trust the live market more directly than in that match.
ALG_BIMODAL_OUTPUT: Algeria's attacking stats swing wildly with opponent quality — their TRUE consistent trait is low fouls, not their SOT/corner numbers, which are unreliable as a base rate here.

questions.text: Will Algeria commit more fouls than Austria?
questions.reasoning: Raw normal-approx (ALG 7.0 vs AUT 12.5) gives 0.06 — extreme, but unlike the Jordan/Algeria foul-script lesson, Algeria's low-foul trait held up in BOTH a blowout loss (8 fouls vs Argentina) AND a dominant win (6 fouls vs Jordan), suggesting it's a genuine team trait, not just a possession-script artifact. Nudge up modestly from the extreme raw number purely for the uncertainty of an even, high-stakes match.

questions.text: Will Austria have more shots on target than Algeria in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Half-split Poisson off full-match averages (AUT λ_2H=1.25, ALG λ_2H=2.25) gives 0.20 raw, but Algeria's SOT average is skewed by one outlier blowout-win game (8 SOT vs Jordan) that may not represent their output in an even, cagey match like today. Nudge up from the raw extreme to reflect that uncertainty.

questions.text: Will Austria score the first goal of the second half?
questions.reasoning: Poisson-race approximation using market-calibrated 2H goal rates for each team (λ_AUT_2H≈0.48, λ_ALG_2H≈0.49 — nearly identical) gives a near-even split, consistent with the overall match parity (47% draw probability).

questions.text: Will Austria score more goals than Algeria in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Skellam off the same market-calibrated 2H lambdas (0.48 vs 0.49) gives 0.26 once tie probability is removed — consistent with a low-scoring, even second half being the most likely outcome.

questions.text: Will Austria receive more cards than Algeria?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Most cards, Austria = 35.37% (essentially identical to Algeria's 35.38%). Trust it — and given the ARG-AUT lesson that competitive matches produce MORE physical play than a dominant-win comparison would suggest, this even split looks well-calibrated rather than an underestimate.
Smarkets FTR: Algeria 26.50%. Direct tier-1 market in the most evenly-matched fixture of the day.

questions.text: Will there be 9 or more total corner kicks?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Over8.5 corners = 30.29%. Trust the live market directly here (unlike CRO-GHA, this is already a matchup-specific live price, not a thin historical sample needing correction).

questions.text: Will Austria have 5 or more shots on target?
questions.reasoning: Market-implied lambda from the Over3.5 SOT line (51.59%→λ=3.75) gives P(≥5)=0.32.

questions.text: Will Marcel Sabitzer have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Liquid market mid (bid 0.25, offer 0.4386) = 0.344. Unlike ARG-AUT, Austria is NOT an extreme underdog today (25.8% win prob, nearly identical to Algeria's 26.5%) — the RULE15-failure lesson applies directly: do not suppress Sabitzer's output in anything resembling a competitive/even match. Trust the liquid market.

questions.text: Will Algeria have 3 or more shots on target?
questions.reasoning: Market-implied lambda from the Over3.5 SOT line (41.67%→λ=3.29) gives P(≥3)=0.64.

strongest_picks: Q1: Algeria more fouls = 0.18 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — genuine team trait confirmed in both a blowout loss AND a dominant win)
Q9: Sabitzer SOT = 0.36 (MEDIUM-HIGH — direct corrective application of the RULE15-failure lesson, recognizing today's genuinely even matchup)
